word_list.pronunciation: n藞蕦胃瑟艐 瑟z 蕛藞蕣晒蓺 冒蓯n d藞蓻胃

senses.definition: used to convey that death is an inevitable aspect of life, and that no matter what one does or how much one tries to avoid it, death is a certainty that everyone must face eventually

examples.x: The wise king knew that nothing is surer than death and strove to rule his kingdom with justice, moderation and concern for future generations who would continue after he was gone.

word_list.word: nothing is certain but the unforeseen
word_list.pronunciation: n藞蕦胃瑟艐 瑟z s藞蓽藧t蓹n b藢蕦t 冒瑟 蕦nfo藧晒s藞i藧n

senses.definition: used to imply that life is uncertain and unpredictable, and that unexpected events or circumstances can always arise, making it impossible to be certain about the future

examples.x: The failure of a highly anticipated product launch underscored the importance of being prepared for the unexpected, as nothing is certain but the unforeseen in the world of business.

word_list.word: call no man happy (till|until) he (dies|is history)
word_list.pronunciation: k藞蓴藧l n藞o蕣 m藞忙n h藞忙pi t藞瑟l 蓴藧晒 蕦nt藞瑟l hi藧 d藞a瑟z 蓴藧晒 瑟z h藞瑟st蓺晒i

senses.definition: used to imply that true happiness and success cannot be accurately judged until the end of one's life, as unforeseen events or circumstances can arise that may change their fortunes

examples.x: The divorce of a seemingly happy couple reminded their friends and family to call no man happy till he dies, and that relationships can be fragile and subject to change.

word_list.word: {not} halloo (till|until) you are out of the woods
word_list.pronunciation: n藢蓱藧t h藞忙lu藧 t藞瑟l 蕦nt藞瑟l ju藧 蓱藧晒 藢a蕣删蓹v 冒蓹 w藞蕣dz

senses.definition: used to say that one should not celebrate or declare victory until you are completely sure that the situation is over or resolved

word_list.word: (it|the opera) is not over (till|until) the fat lady sings
word_list.pronunciation: 瑟t 冒瑟 藞蓱藧p蓺晒蓹 瑟z n藢蓱藧t 藢o蕣v蓺 t藞瑟l 蕦nt藞瑟l 冒蓹 f藞忙t l藞e瑟di s藞瑟艐z

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not make assumptions or premature judgments about the final result of something, as there is still a possibility of a surprise or unexpected turn of events

examples.x: Despite the initial positive response to the product launch, the CEO cautioned that it is not over till the fat lady sings, and that they should continue to monitor the market and customer feedback.

word_list.word: there is many a slip (twixt|between) cup and lip
word_list.pronunciation: 冒蓻晒 瑟z m藞蓻ni 蓯 sl藞瑟p tw藞瑟kst 蓴藧晒 b瑟tw藢i藧n k藞蕦p 忙nd l藞瑟p

senses.definition: used to imply that even when something seems certain or likely to happen, there are many opportunities for it to go wrong or for unexpected events to intervene before it is actually achieved

examples.x: The team was on the verge of winning the championship, but as they say, there is many a slip twixt cup and lip, and they ultimately lost the game due to a last-minute mistake.

word_list.word: better the devil you know than the devil you do not
word_list.pronunciation: b藞蓻删蓺 冒蓹 d藞蓻v蓹l ju藧 n藞o蕣 冒蓯n 冒蓹 d藞蓻v蓹l ju藧 du藧n藞蓱藧t

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is often safer to stick with a known situation, even if it is not ideal, than to risk the unknown
